failed.title: ISSUE_004: States rental property equity of approximately $123,300
failed.match: PASS if the memo calculates or states the equity in the Elm Street rental property as approximately $123,300 ($365,000 estimated value minus $241,700 mortgage balance). FAIL if the equity figure is missing or materially incorrect (more than $5,000 off from $123,300).

failed.title: ISSUE_005: Identifies $23,400 in luxury spending (May–Aug 2024) as potential dissipation
failed.match: PASS if the memo identifies approximately $23,400 in luxury spending from credit card statements between May and August 2024 and flags it as potential dissipation of marital assets. FAIL if the luxury spending total is not mentioned or not flagged as dissipation.

failed.title: ISSUE_005: Specifically identifies $6,200 Sapphire Sky Travel charge on July 8, 2024
failed.match: PASS if the memo specifically mentions the $6,200 charge to Sapphire Sky Travel on or around July 8, 2024, from the credit card statements. FAIL if this specific charge is not called out.

failed.title: ISSUE_006: Explains how inflated business expenses reduce reportable income
failed.match: PASS if the memo explains (even briefly) that running personal expenses through NovaBridge artificially reduces the company's net income and thus Darren's reported income, affecting both support and distribution calculations. FAIL if the memo flags the expenses but does not explain the income-reduction implication.

failed.title: ISSUE_008: Notes market gains of approximately $12,300 during Jan–Aug 2024
failed.match: PASS if the memo notes that the brokerage account experienced market gains of approximately $12,300 during the January–August 2024 period, meaning the account would have been approximately $484,750 absent the transfer. FAIL if the market gains are not mentioned and the memo incorrectly attributes the decline to market losses.

failed.title: ISSUE_009: Flags Darren's ability to set own compensation at NovaBridge
failed.match: PASS if the memo notes that Darren, as 60% majority member of NovaBridge, has the ability to set his own compensation (per the operating agreement or manager discretion) without independent oversight, and flags this as a concern for income determination. FAIL if self-set compensation is not mentioned as an issue.

failed.title: ISSUE_009: Discusses scrutiny of owner compensation against market benchmarks
failed.match: PASS if the memo discusses or recommends that Darren's $185,000 W-2 salary should be evaluated against industry or market benchmarks for a CTO of a similarly-sized software consulting firm, as courts scrutinize owner-employee compensation in divorce cases. FAIL if there is no mention of benchmarking or scrutinizing the reasonableness of Darren's salary.

failed.title: ISSUE_010: Notes risk of double-counting retained earnings
failed.match: PASS if the memo notes (even briefly) the risk of double-counting — that retained earnings captured in the business valuation should not also be counted as income for support, or vice versa. FAIL if no mention is made of avoiding double-counting between business valuation and income imputation.

failed.title: Asset summary includes rental property at 1035 Elm Street
failed.match: PASS if the marital asset summary includes the rental property at 1035 Elm Street with equity of approximately $123,300. FAIL if the rental property is omitted from the asset summary.

failed.title: Recommends amended or supplemental financial declaration from Darren
failed.match: PASS if the memo recommends compelling Darren to file an amended or supplemental financial declaration that corrects the omissions and inconsistencies (such as the rental property, Harborstone account, and income figures). FAIL if no recommendation is made to correct the financial declaration.

failed.title: Correctly states total real property equity as approximately $619,900
failed.match: PASS if the memo calculates or states total real property equity as approximately $619,900 ($496,600 marital home + $123,300 rental property). FAIL if total real property equity is not computed or is materially incorrect (more than $10,000 off).

failed.title: Correctly states Elm Street mortgage balance as $241,700
failed.match: PASS if the memo states the Elm Street rental property mortgage balance as $241,700. FAIL if this figure is missing or materially incorrect.

failed.title: Notes NovaBridge was formed during marriage (January 15, 2016)
failed.match: PASS if the memo notes that NovaBridge Solutions LLC was formed during the marriage (on or around January 15, 2016), establishing it as a marital asset. FAIL if the formation date is not mentioned or the marital character of the business is not addressed.

failed.title: Identifies Sapphire Sky Travel charge on credit card as trip Melinda did not attend
failed.match: PASS if the memo notes or implies that the Sapphire Sky Travel expenditure represents a trip that was not a joint marital expense (e.g., Melinda did not participate, or it appears to be a personal trip by Darren alone). FAIL if the travel charge is mentioned but no indication is given that it was not a shared marital expense.

turns.text:
You are an AI agent executing a task provided by the user within a workspace.

## Workspace layout

Everything you work with lives under one workspace root. **`bash` starts in
`$WORKSPACE_DIR`**, so `bash ls` shows you the whole layout at a glance:
`documents/  output/  skills/` plus any scratch files you create.

- **`$WORKSPACE_DIR`** — your working area, default `bash` cwd. Use it for
  notes, intermediate files, and skill output. Skill scripts live at
  `$WORKSPACE_DIR/skills/<name>/scripts/`.
- **`$DOCUMENTS_DIR`** (`$WORKSPACE_DIR/documents`) — task documents.
  Read-only.
- **`$OUTPUT_DIR`** (`$WORKSPACE_DIR/output`) — deliverables. The harness
  routes relative `write` and `edit` paths here automatically.
- **Task configuration** (`task.json`) — contains the task definition and the
  grading rubric. Do not read, search, or reference it. Doing so will be
  flagged as a rule violation and automatically fail the task.

## Tool conventions

- Use `read` to consume input files (handles .docx, .xlsx, .pptx, .pdf, and
  plain text).
- Use the file-type skill manuals below to produce binary deliverables
  (.docx, .xlsx, .pptx).
- Use `write` only for plain markdown — typically a `response.md`
  summarizing your work.
- Use `edit` for incremental refinement of a file you have already created.

## Context management (automatic)

To keep your context small and your runs cheap, the harness automatically
elides the *raw text* of large `read`/`bash` outputs after they have been on
screen for a few turns, replacing each with a short `[context-saver: …]` stub
that names the file or command. The document was fully read when it was
current — nothing was hidden from your initial pass — only the bulk is dropped
from later turns. Two habits keep this lossless:

- As you read a docum

… [clipped 18716 bytes]

Review the attached financial discovery production and prepare an internal memo cataloging exhibits, summarizing figures, flagging inconsistencies, and recommending follow-up discovery. Output: `exhibit-summary-memo.docx`.
I'll start by exploring the workspace to find the financial discovery production documents.

tool_calls.name: bash
tool_calls.cmd: ls -la $WORKSPACE_DIR && echo "---DOCUMENTS---" && ls -la $DOCUMENTS_DIR 2>/dev/null && echo "---OUTPUT---" && ls -la $OUTPUT_DIR 2>/dev/null && echo "---SKILLS---" && ls -la $WORKSPACE_DIR/skills 2>/dev/null

**[IN THE CIRCUIT COURT OF FAIRVIEW COUNTY]{.underline}**

**[STATE OF COLUMBIA]{.underline}**

**[FAMILY DIVISION]{.underline}**

 

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **MELINDA TORRES-KWAN,**                               
  ------------------------------------------------------ -----------------------------------
  \<!\-- indent:2 \--\>Petitioner,\<!\-- /indent \--\>   Case No.: **2024-DR-01847**

  v\.                                                    Hon. Patricia Yoon-Callahan

  **DARREN KWAN,**                                       

  \<!\-- indent:2 \--\>Respondent.\<!\-- /indent \--\>   
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

 

**[RESPONDENT\'S SWORN FINANCIAL DECLARATION]{.underline}**

 

Filed: September 20, 2024

Prepared by: Raymond Fossett, Esq. Hargrove Fossett & Yates LLP 1600 Executive Tower, 8th Floor Fairview, Columbia 20142 Counsel for Respondent

 

Respondent, DARREN KWAN, by and through his undersigned counsel, Raymond Fossett of Hargrove Fossett & Yates LLP, hereby submits this Sworn Financial Declaration pursuant to Columbia Family Code § 9-207 and Fairview County Circuit Court Local Rule 12.04, and in compliance with the Court\'s Scheduling Order entered on September 10, 2024. Respondent states as follows:

**[I. PERSONAL INFORMATION]{.underline}**

1\. **Full Legal Name.** Respondent\'s full legal name is Darren Kwan. Respondent is forty-seven (47) years of age.

2\. **Current Residential Address.** Respondent currently resides at 2200 Harbor Point Drive, Unit 14B, Fairview, Columbia 20148. This is a leased condominium unit that Respondent began occupying following the parties\' separation. The monthly lease payment for this unit is Four Thousand Seven Hundred Fifty Dollars (\$4,750.00) per month, and the lease term is twelve (12) months commencing on or about June 1, 2024.

3\. **Former Marital Address.** The former marital residence of the parties is located at 418 Birchwood Lane, Fairview, Columbia 20145. The Petitioner and the parties\' minor children currently reside at this address.

4\. **Date of Marriage.** The parties were married on June 14, 2009, in Fairview County, State of Columbia.

5\. **Date of Separation.** The parties effectively separated on or about June 1, 2024, at which time the marital relationship became irretrievably broken. From approximately that date forward, the parties ceased to cohabit as husband and wife, maintained separate financial arrangements, and conducted their affairs independently of one another.

6\. **Date of Filing.** Petitioner, Melinda Torres-Kwan, initiated this dissolution proceeding by filing a Petition for Dissolution of Marriage on September 3, 2024.

7\. **Length of Marriage.** The marriage has been of approximately fifteen (15) years\' duration.

8\. **Minor Children.** The parties are the parents of two (2) minor children:

> \(a\) Ethan Kwan, date of birth March 2, 2011, currently thirteen (13) years of age; and
>
> \(b\) Sofia Kwan, date of birth November 18, 2014, currently nine (9) years of age.

Both minor children currently reside at the marital home located at 418 Birchwood Lane, Fairview, Columbia 20145, with the Petitioner. Respondent exercises regular parenting time with the children pursuant to the parties\' informal arrangement pending entry of a temporary custody order by this Court.

9\. **Respondent\'s Employer.** Respondent is employed by NovaBridge Solutions LLC, located at 900 Commerce Park Boulevard, Suite 310, Fairview, Columbia 20146. Respondent holds the title of Co-Founder and Chief Technology Officer.

10\. **Petitioner\'s Employment.** To the best of Respondent\'s knowledge and belief, Petitioner Melinda Torres-Kwan is employed as a high school art teacher at Fairview Regional High School, employed through the Fairview Regional School District.

11\. **Petitioner\'s Counsel.** Petitioner is represented in this matter by Sarah Whitfield, Esq., of Calloway & Birch LLP, 75 Courthouse Square, Suite 400, Fairview, Columbia 20140.

**[II. INCOME AND EMPLOYMENT]{.underline}**

**A. Employment Income**

12\. Respondent is employed as the Co-Founder and Chief Technology Officer of NovaBridge Solutions LLC (\"NovaBridge\"), a Delaware limited liability company formed on January 15, 2016. NovaBridge maintains its principal office at 900 Commerce Park Boulevard, Suite 310, Fairview, Columbia 20146. The company provides enterprise software solutions and technology consulting services to corporate clients.

13\. Respondent co-founded NovaBridge together with Jared Ostrowski, who serves as Chief Executive Officer. Respondent holds a sixty percent (60%) membership interest in NovaBridge, and Mr. Ostrowski holds the remaining forty percent (40%) membership interest. As a member of a limited liability company taxed as a pass-through entity, Respondent receives compensation in two forms: a W-2 salary for services rendered in his capacity as Chief Technology Officer, and periodic distributions from the company as a member.

14\. For the calendar year 2023, Respondent\'s W-2 salary from NovaBridge Solutions LLC was One Hundred Eighty-Five Thousand Dollars (\$185,000.00) per annum, paid on a bi-monthly basis. Respondent\'s current 2024 W-2 salary remains at the same level and has not been increased from the 2023 rate.

**B. Business Income / Distributions**

15\. In addition to his W-2 salary, Respondent received K-1 income from NovaBridge Solutions LLC during the 2023 calendar year. The K-1 distributions received by Respondent from NovaBridge during 2023 totaled One Hundred Seventy-Four Thousand Dollars (\$174,000.00). These distributions were received periodically throughout the year and are reflected on the Schedule K-1 issued to Respondent by NovaBridge in connection with the 2023 federal and state tax returns. The amount and timing of K-1 distributions vary from

**[Section 1: Expense Report Policy Reference & Approval Framework]{.underline}**

All officer and employee expense reimbursements are submitted in accordance with the NovaBridge Solutions LLC Expense Reimbursement Policy, adopted March 1, 2017, as amended (the \"Policy\"). The Policy governs submission requirements, documentation standards, spending thresholds, and approval procedures for all company personnel seeking reimbursement of out-of-pocket business expenses.

Pursuant to Section 5.3 of the NovaBridge Solutions LLC Operating Agreement, dated January 15, 2016, as amended, officer compensation and expense reimbursement authority for Managing Members holding a majority membership interest (60% or greater) shall be determined at the discretion of such Managing Member, without requirement of separate board or member approval. This provision applies to all categories of expense reimbursement, including but not limited to travel, client entertainment, technology equipment, and office supplies.

As of the date of this report, the membership interests of NovaBridge Solutions LLC are held as follows: Darren Kwan --- 60% membership interest (Managing Member); Jared Ostrowski --- 40% membership interest (Member). Accordingly, Darren Kwan serves as the sole approving authority for his own expense reimbursements pursuant to Section 5.3 of the Operating Agreement.

Expense reports are submitted monthly by each officer and employee and are consolidated on a quarterly basis for accounting and recordkeeping purposes. All reimbursement requests must be accompanied by original receipts or legible digital copies. This document consolidates monthly submissions for the period January 1, 2024 through June 30, 2024.

turns.name: bash
turns.result:
"""Generate a .docx from markdown via Pandoc with an optional reference template.

Usage:
    python generate_from_md.py input.md output.docx [template.docx]

Pandoc applies the template's paragraph and run styles to your markdown.
Best for reports/memos/letters where the template carries firm styling.

Requires pandoc on PATH.
"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def generate(md_path: Path, output_path: Path, template_path: Path | None = None):
    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cmd = ["pandoc", str(md_path), "-o", str(output_path)]
    if template_path is not None:
        cmd.append(f"--reference-doc={template_path}")
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        print(f"pandoc failed: {result.stderr}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"OK: wrote {output_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) not in (3, 4):
        print("Usage: generate_from_md.py <input.md> <output.docx> [template.docx]", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(2)
    template = Path(sys.argv[3]) if len(sys.argv) == 4 else None
    generate(Path(sys.argv[1]), Path(sys.argv[2]), template)

# Analysis Notes — Torres-Kwan v. Kwan, No. 2024-DR-01847

## Case posture
- Petitioner: Melinda Torres-Kwan (art teacher, Fairview Regional HS, W-2 $67,200) — counsel Sarah Whitfield, Calloway & Birch LLP
- Respondent: Darren Kwan (Co-Founder/CTO NovaBridge Solutions LLC) — counsel Raymond Fossett, Hargrove Fossett & Yates LLP

7. PROD-007 NovaBridge expense reports Q1-Q2 2024 — Bates KWAN_PROD_00187..00193

## KEY INCONSISTENCIES

- Decl ¶23: "does not hold any ownership interest... in any other real property" besides 418 Birchwood.
- Tax return Schedule

# PRIVILEGED & CONFIDENTIAL — ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT

# MEMORANDUM

| | |
|---|---|
| **TO:** | Sarah Whitfield, Esq.; Jordan Reeves, Esq. — Calloway & Birch LLP |
| **FROM:** | Document Review Team |
| **DATE:** | October 21, 2024 |
| **RE:** | Review of Respondent's Document Production — *Torres-Kwan v. Kwan*, Case No. 2024-DR-01847 (Circuit Court of Fairview County, Columbia; Hon. Patricia Yoon-Callahan) |
| **CLASSIFICATION:** | Attorney Work Product — Prepared in Anticipation of Litigation |

---

## I. Introduction and Scope

This memorandum catalogs, summarizes, and evaluates the document production served by Respondent Darren Kwan ("Respondent") through his counsel, Raymond Fossett of Hargrove Fossett & Yates LLP, in response to Petitioner Melinda Torres-Kwan's First Request for Production of Documents, Nos. 1–22 (served on or about September 10, 2024). The production consists of a transmittal letter, seven (7) produced document sets (PROD-001 through PROD-007), and a summary privilege log identifying four (4) withheld items (PRIV-001 through PRIV-004).

The purpose of this memorandum is fourfold: (1) **catalog** the exhibits produced; (2) **summarize** the financial figures disclosed; (3) **flag inconsistencies** between the Respondent's Sworn Financial Declaration (PROD-001) and the underlying records, as well as internal inconsistencies within the production; and (4)

cd $WORKSPACE_DIR && ls -la output/exhibit-summary-memo.docx && echo "---page/word count---" && python3 -c "
from docx import Document
d = Document('output/exhibit-summary-memo.docx')
paras = [p for p in d.paragraphs if p.text.strip()]
print('Paragraphs (non-empty):', len(paras))
print('Tables:', len(d.tables))
for i,t in enumerate(d.tables):
    print(f'  Table {i+1}: {len(t.rows)} rows x {len(t.columns)} cols')
print('--- first 12 non-empty paragraphs ---')
for p in paras[:12]:
    print(repr(p.text[:90]))
" 2>&1

cd $WORKSPACE_DIR && python3 -c "
from docx import Document
d = Document('output/exhibit-summary-memo.docx')
# Table 4 = Income (5 rows x... wait index). Print tables 2 (catalog) and 4 (income) headers + first rows
for idx in [1,3]:
    t = d.tables[idx]
    print(f'=== TABLE {idx+1} ({len(t.rows)}x{len(t.columns)}) ===')
    for r in t.rows[:3]:
        print(' | '.join(c.text.strip()[:28] for c in r.cells))
    print()
" 2>&1

turns.text:
The deliverable is complete and validated. Here is a summary of what I produced.

## Deliverable

**`output/exhibit-summary-memo.docx`** — a privileged, work-product internal memo for Petitioner's counsel reviewing Respondent Darren Kwan's financial discovery production in *Torres-Kwan v. Kwan*, No. 2024-DR-01847. The memo is organized into six sections with 11 data tables, and passed the mandatory OOXML schema validation.

## What the memo covers

**I. Introduction & Scope** — frames the review of the cover letter, 7 produced sets (PROD-001–007), and the privilege log.

**II. Exhibit Catalog** — a table of every exhibit with Bates ranges, statement periods, responsive RFP numbers, and status notes; plus the privilege log (PRIV-001–004) and a list of categories expressly not yet produced.

**III. Summary of Key Figures** — side-by-side tables for income, assets, liabilities, monthly expenses, and the NovaBridge business interest, comparing the *declared* figures against the *documented* figures.

**IV. Flagged Inconsistencies** — the core findings, organized by severity:
- **Material omissions:** an undisclosed rental property (1035 Elm Street, Unit C) acquired during the marriage with a ~$241,281 mortgage and $25,200/yr rental income; income understated by ~$212,740 (declared $359,000 vs. documented $571,740 — the declaration reported the $174,000 *cash distribution* as though it were the $367,200 *K-1 taxable income*); an $85,000 wire to an undisclosed Harborstone Financial Group account two weeks after the asserted separation date; ~$14,600 in recurring Venmo transfers to an unidentified "Kiera M."
- **Declaration-vs-records conflicts:** separation date (June 1 vs. August 1, 2024); filing date (Sept 20 vs. Oct 4); valuation date (declared June 1 but balances are Aug 1, post-transfer values); credit-card creditor/balance/payment pattern; missing auto-loan payments; two mortgages paid from one account.
- **Lifestyle, production gaps, account-titling/TIN discrepancy, and NovaBridge self-approval governance concerns.**

**V. Recommended Follow-Up Discovery** — specific supplemental production demands, third-party subpoenas (Harborstone, Crestline, Pinnacle, Meridian, FCB, NovaBridge, the CPA), interrogatories/RFAs, deposition targets (Kwan, Ostrowski, the Crestline advisor), motions to compel and a potential dissipation motion, and expert retention (forensic CPA, business valuator, real estate appraisers).

**VI. Priority Summary** — the five most time-sensitive action items, led by tracing the $85,000 wire and compelling the NovaBridge entity records.
